claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each child individually.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for one child - my son Alex. He's 7 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Your son Alex is 7 years old, which means he's under 16. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Since Alex is 7 years old and under 16, he automatically qualifies as far as age is concerned. Now I need to ask about your circumstances:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This means does he live with you, or if he doesn't live with you, are you contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards his upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his other parent. But I do contribute towards his upkeep - I help out with money for his clothes, school things, and other expenses.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. This is an important point. For you to be eligible for Child Benefit when Alex doesn't live with you, you need to be contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Let me ask you more specifically: **Do you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards Alex's upkeep each week?** 

Child Benefit is currently paid at a weekly rate (around £25 per week for the eldest or only child, though you should check the current rates on gov.uk). Are your contributions at least this much per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute £50 a week towards Alex's upkeep, so that's well above the Child Benefit amount.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. However, I need to clarify an important rule about Child Benefit when a child doesn't live with you.

Even though you're contributing more than the Child Benefit amount, **only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child**. If Alex's other parent (the one he lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for him, then you cannot also claim it.

**Is Alex's other parent currently claiming Child Benefit for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, his mum is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex since he lives with her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility.

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**. Here's why:

**The key rule is that only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child at any one time.** Since Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him, you cannot also claim it - even though you are contributing financially to his upkeep.

The person who the child lives with has priority for claiming Child Benefit. While your financial contributions are commendable and important for Alex's wellbeing, they don't enable you to claim Child Benefit when someone else is already receiving it for the same child.

**What this means:**
- Alex's mother will continue to receive the Child Benefit payments for him
- You cannot make a separate claim for Child Benefit for Alex while she is claiming it
- If circumstances change in the future (for example, if Alex comes to live with you or his mother stops claiming), you could potentially claim at that point

Is there anything else about Child Benefit that you'd like me to clarify?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's clear. Thank you for explaining it to me. I understand now that since his mum is already claiming it and he lives with her, I'm not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
